fact: May 8 is exactly 128 days into the year - that's exactly 2 to the 7th power!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for baby rabbits to be hopping around - they're about 3 weeks old and super fluffy!
Dandelions are going absolutely crazy right now, shooting their fuzzy seed parachutes everywhere when you blow on them.
The sun rises before 6 AM in most of North America around this date, which means it's getting light while you're still sleeping!
May 8 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season - the stubborn bull of the zodiac that loves food and naps.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are turning orange and red right now as autumn kicks in.
Earthworms are having a party in the soil because it's warm enough but still moist from spring rains - perfect worm weather!
This date sits exactly 44 days after the spring equinox, when day and night were perfectly equal.
Mosquitoes are just starting to hatch from their winter hiding spots in warmer climates - get ready for itchy summer!
The sunset around May 8 happens after 7:30 PM in most northern cities, giving you extra playground time after dinner.
Lilac bushes are exploding with purple flowers that smell so strong you can sniff them from across the yard.
May 8 has exactly 237 days left in the year - that's more than 7 months of fun stuff still coming!
